2603.19002.title: RADIUS: Ranking, Distribution, and Significance - A Comprehensive Alignment Suite for Survey Simulation
2603.19002.paper_summary: Simulation of surveys using LLMs is emerging as a powerful application for generating human-like responses at scale. Prior work evaluates survey simulation using metrics borrowed from other domains, which are often ad hoc, fragmented, and non-standardized, leading to results that are difficult to compare. Moreover, existing metrics focus mainly on accuracy or distributional measures, overlooking the critical dimension of ranking alignment. In practice, a simulation can achieve high accuracy while still failing to capture the option most preferred by humans - a distinction that is critical in decision-making applications. We introduce RADIUS, a comprehensive two-dimensional alignment suite for survey simulation that captures: 1) RAnking alignment and 2) DIstribUtion alignment, each complemented by statistical Significance testing. RADIUS highlights the limitations of existing metrics, enables more meaningful evaluation of survey simulation, and provides an open-source implementation for reproducible and comparable assessment.
2603.19002.paper_summary_zh: 模擬使用 LLM 的調查正在成為一種強大的應用，用於大規模生成類似人類的回應。先前的研究使用從其他領域借來的指標來評估調查模擬，這些指標往往是臨時的、零散的且不標準化，導致結果難以比較。此外，現有指標主要集中在準確性或分佈性度量上，忽略了排名一致性這一關鍵維度。在實踐中，模擬可以達到高準確性，但仍然無法捕捉到人類最偏好的選項——這一區別在決策應用中至關重要。我們介紹了 RADIUS，一個全面的二維對齊套件，用於調查模擬，捕捉：1）排名對齊和 2）分佈對齊，每一項都輔以統計顯著性檢驗。RADIUS 突出了現有指標的局限性，使調查模擬的評估更加有意義，並提供了開源實現，以便進行可重現和可比較的評估。
2603.19002.author: Weronika Łajewska et.al.
2603.19002.authors: Weronika Łajewska, Paul Missault, George Davidson, Saab Mansour
